frontier.hypothesis: The g3-basin residual (masked=6, pure v1<->a0 register exchange, order-perfect) admits a novel closing lever via `register` pin manipulations that were s1/WIP-KILLED on h5-basin but were NEVER re-measured on g3-basin under the 2026-07-06 do-while(0)-sanction + 2026-07-01 dead-store/named-local carve-outs. Specifically: a fn-scope BLOCK-LOCAL multi-set carrier for arg5 wrapped in a sanctioned do{}while(0) whose LOOP_BEG/END ref-multiplier lifts arg5-qty refs*loop-depth above t0-qty on the g3 chassis (where s5 measured wraps as monotonically wrong-direction ONLY on h5-basin, never on g3-basin).
frontier.mechanism: s5's F3/F3b/F4 do-while(0) wraps were all measured on h5 base and were monotonically wrong-direction. But g3's ORDER-PERFECT status means wrap-based ref-reweighting on g3 doesn't have to compete with a pair-swap residual — only with the v1/a0 register exchange. flow.c reg_n_refs multiplied by loop depth feeds local-alloc.c qty_compare. Untested cross: wrap around ONLY the arg5 dereference statement on g3 base.
frontier.next_probe: Structural modality (next session): Apply g3 base to src/system.c (t0<<=2, arg5=*(s32*)(v0+tbl); t0<<=2; call(..., *(s32*)(t0+tbl_125c), arg5) — no *=4 mult-expander; masked=6 baseline). Wrap ONLY `arg5 = *(s32*)(v0 + (s32)tbl_125c);` in do{}while(0). Sandbox --disable all; if masked drops below 6, then wrap the arg5 chain more aggressively (v0<<=2; arg5=...) OR try the fully-inline arg5 in-call form with wrap. If INERT at 6, wrap-based ref-lift on g3 is closed too.

frontier.hypothesis: PERM_LINESWAP_TEXT (line-level, text-based swap that handles incomplete statements) applied to the ENTIRE do_timeout block INCLUDING the do_timeout label and tslTm2LoadImage_2 call could discover a sanctioned control-flow ordering that s22 PERM_LINESWAP (AST-based, block-scoped) could not reach.
frontier.mechanism: PERM_LINESWAP operates on complete C statements at AST level within one block scope; it cannot swap lines across the do_timeout label boundary or interleave the tslTm2LoadImage_2 call with the inline block. PERM_LINESWAP_TEXT is textual and could reach broader orderings, some of which might re-time the block-3 sched.c ready queue in ways that break the LUID(121)>LUID(111) tiebreak without disturbing basin membership.
frontier.next_probe: Permuter modality (later session): edit tmp/perm_csmd4/base.c with PERM_LINESWAP_TEXT wrapping tslTm2LoadImage_2(...) + the whole inline block; verify base_score=60 at launch (must preserve h5). Cap fresh-seed harvest at ~20 min OR until textual-mutation-space exhausts (much larger than 5040). Guard: any find with weighted score >= 60 that produces masked < 2 is the target; forms in g3 basin (weighted 40) are dupes of s22 findings.

frontier.hypothesis: The forensic surface has ONE unexplored micro-axis: dropping BOTH v0 and t0 chain statements at the same LUID slot by co-declaring them and using an assignment operator that produces a comma-separated expression sequence, could give sched.c a novel adjust_priority interaction between p106 (t0*=4 fresh dest) and p107 (arg5_addr). Not measured in any prior session.
frontier.mechanism: GCC's expand-time comma expression lowering may create a distinct RTL insertion point relative to statement-order; if p106's SET INSN and p107's SET INSN end up at the SAME LUID (via a comma expression tying their computes into a single sequence-point), the LUID tiebreak degenerates and adjust_priority may fall through to a different comparator (register class, pri) that hasn't been probed.
frontier.next_probe: Structural modality: rewrite the inline block to use comma-op `v0 <<= 2, arg5 = *(s32*)(v0+tbl)`, `t0 *= 4, t0 = (u8*)tbl+t0` folded into single statements; if sched.c assigns identical LUID to the SLL emissions, rank_for_schedule's val=0 outcome may shift class or fall to REGNO. Measure via sandbox; if INERT, comma-op axis is closed.
